Issue: Grad state not restored on exception in FgImageStats and LabelStats
Decision: Wrapped computation body in try/finally in FgImageStats.__call__ and LabelStats.__call__
Rationale: Exception safety requires restore_grad_state to execute regardless of exceptions; try/finally ensures this
Author: williams145
Description: FgImageStats.__call__ and LabelStats.__call__ methods in monai/auto3dseg/analyzer.py
Time stamp: 2026-04-08T16:53:28Z (Created At), 2026-04-08T16:53:48Z (Comment by coderabbitai[bot]), 2026-04-09T16:12:38Z (Comment by williams145), 2026-04-09T21:52:23Z (Comment by williams145), 2026-04-13T21:54:36Z (Comment by ericspod), 2026-04-16T20:05:09Z (Comment by williams145), 2026-04-16T21:43:36Z (Comment by ericspod), 2026-04-17T09:03:10Z (Comment by williams145), 2026-04-18T20:24:59Z (Comment by williams145)
Cost: Low (minimal code change, no new dependencies)
Risk: Technical debt: exception-unsafe gradient state handling could silently break downstream computations

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: torch.no_grad() would fix this equally well; the try/finally pattern is equivalent but makes save/restore intent explicit

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: The actual bug is that raw torch.set_grad_enabled(False) with restore only on happy path leaves grad permanently disabled on exception; torch.no_grad() would fix this equally well

Justification: The issue pertains to exception safety in gradient state restoration (FgImageStats.__call__, LabelStats.__call__), which is a general software engineering practice rather than a core ML lifecycle task. Keywords like 'grad state not restored' and 'exception safety' indicate environment/service-level concerns.
